turns.en: My concern isn't the long run. It's the next 3 to seven years. How do we head towards Star Trek and not Terminator?
turns.zh: 我担心的不是长期，而是未来三到七年。我们如何走向《星际迷航》而不是《终结者》？

turns.en: I call AI and robotics the supersonic tsunami. We're in the singularity.
turns.zh: 我把 AI 和机器人称为超音速海啸。我们正处于奇点之中。

turns.en: When is all white by color work gone? Anything short of shaping atoms. AI can do half or more of those jobs right now. There's no on-off switch. It is coming and accelerating. The transition will be bumpy. You have a solution to this.
turns.zh: 什么时候所有白领工作都会消失？除了塑造原子之外的一切。AI 现在就能完成一半以上的这些工作。没有开关。它正在到来并且加速。过渡会很坎坷。你有解决方案。

turns.en: I don't make a bet here.
turns.zh: 我不在这里下注。

turns.en: China's done an incredible job, right? I mean, it's running circles around us. Do you imagine that the US could make that level of investment and commitment based on current trends?
turns.zh: 中国做得非常出色，对吧？我的意思是，它远远领先于我们。你觉得美国基于当前趋势能做出那种水平的投资和承诺吗？

turns.en: China will far exceed the rest of the world in AI compute.
turns.zh: 中国在 AI 算力上将远远超过世界其他地区。

turns.en: Every major CEO and economist and government leader should be like, what do we do? We don't have any system right now to make this go well. But AI is a critical part of making it go well. There are three things that I think are important. Truth will prevent AI from going insane. Curiosity, I think, will foster any form of sentience. And if it has a sense of beauty, it will be a great future. It's going to be an awesome future.
turns.zh: 每一位重要的 CEO、经济学家和政府领导人都应该问：我们该怎么办？我们现在没有任何系统能让这件事顺利进行。但 AI 是让它顺利进行的关键部分。我认为有三件事很重要。真理会防止 AI 发疯。好奇心，我认为，会培养任何形式的感知力。如果它有美感，那将是一个美好的未来。这将是一个了不起的未来。

turns.en: Now, that's a moonshot, ladies and gentlemen. Welcome to Moonshots. Following is a wide-ranging conversation with Elon Musk focused on optimism and the coming age of abundance. My moonshot mate Dave Blondon and I flew into Austin, Texas to meet up with Elon at his 11.5 million square foot Gigafactory, home of the Cybertruck and Model Y production and the future home for 8 million square ft of Optimus production. Elon has agreed to do this kind of a deep dive catchup once per year. This is hopefully the first of many. And after having this conversation with Elon, it's crystal clear to me that we are living through the singularity. All right, enjoy.
turns.zh: 现在，这就是一个登月计划，女士们先生们。欢迎来到《登月计划》。以下是与埃隆·马斯克的广泛对话，聚焦于乐观主义和即将到来的富足时代。我的登月伙伴戴夫·布兰登和我飞往德克萨斯州奥斯汀，在埃隆的 1150 万平方英尺的超级工厂与他见面，这里是 Cybertruck 和 Model Y 的生产地，也是未来 800 万平方英尺 Optimus 生产地。埃隆同意每年进行一次这样的深度交流。希望这是许多次中的第一次。在与埃隆交谈后，我清楚地意识到我们正生活在奇点之中。好了，请欣赏。

turns.en: Your relentless optimism is always a breath of fresh air.
turns.zh: 你坚持不懈的乐观主义总是一股清新的空气。

turns.en: Thank you, buddy. Thank you. Well, I want to share that tonight with a lot of people.
turns.zh: 谢谢，伙计。谢谢。嗯，今晚我想和很多人分享这一点。

turns.en: I think they need it.
turns.zh: 我觉得他们需要它。

turns.en: I hope you're right. And you might be right. Actually, I'm increasingly thinking that you are right.
turns.zh: 我希望你是对的。你可能是对的。实际上，我越来越觉得你是对的。

turns.en: Abundance for all.
turns.zh: 人人富足。

turns.en: That's the goal. Shall we?
turns.zh: 这就是目标。我们开始吧？

turns.en: Right now, putting a lot of time into chips.
turns.zh: 现在，你花了很多时间在芯片上。

turns.en: You are. You are personally.
turns.zh: 是的，你亲自在做。

turns.en: It's always AI assistance, I assume.
turns.zh: 我猜总是有 AI 辅助吧。

turns.en: Not enough.
turns.zh: 还不够。

turns.en: It'd be nice if we could just hand it off to the AI.
turns.zh: 如果能直接交给 AI 就好了。

turns.en: Soon enough.
turns.zh: 很快了。

turns.en: I tried to do some circuit design actually with AI recently. Just a couple weeks ago. Not happening yet.
turns.zh: 我最近确实尝试用 AI 做了一些电路设计。就在几周前。还没成功。

turns.en: Very soon though.
turns.zh: 不过很快了。

turns.en: I think probably at this point Grok, if you took a photo and submitted to Grok, it could probably tell you if there's something wrong with the circuit.
turns.zh: 我想现在 Grok 可能就能做到，如果你拍张照片提交给 Grok，它大概能告诉你电路是否有问题。

turns.en: All right. I'm going to give it a shot. You're using the same Grok that I'm using?
turns.zh: 好吧，我来试试。你用的是和我一样的 Grok 吗？

turns.en: Grok keeps updating. So 4.2, but five is soon, right?
turns.zh: Grok 一直在更新。所以是 4.2，但 5 很快了，对吧？

turns.en: Five is Q1.
turns.zh: 5 在第一季度。

turns.en: 4.2 has not been released yet externally. But yeah, I mean if you just upload an image into Grok, it does quite a good job of analyzing any given image.
turns.zh: 4.2 还没有对外发布。但如果你上传一张图片到 Grok，它分析任何给定图像都做得相当好。

turns.en: Absolutely. Let's start. We're going to talk about this.
turns.zh: 当然。我们开始吧。我们要讨论这个。

turns.en: All right. We'll come back.
turns.zh: 好的。我们回头再说。

turns.en: I mean, let's see if I take a picture of you, what is it? Let's see what it says.
turns.zh: 我的意思是，如果我拍一张你的照片，它会说什么？我们看看。

turns.en: Yeah. What's it going to say about me?
turns.zh: 是啊，它会怎么说我？

turns.en: It's going to say you're a flawed circuit. I also have to remember to update it because we update the Grok app so frequently.
turns.zh: 它会说你是一个有缺陷的电路。我还得记得更新它，因为我们更新 Grok 应用非常频繁。

turns.en: You know, I asked Grok to roast me. It did an amazing job. Then I asked Grok to roast you. And I spit out my coffee. It was hilarious. And then I asked it to say more. It just keeps telling it to be more and more. I asked until it's like mother of God.
turns.zh: 你知道吗，我让 Grok 吐槽我。它做得非常棒。然后我让 Grok 吐槽你。我喷出了咖啡。太搞笑了。然后我让它再说更多。它就一直说越来越狠。我一直问到它说‘我的天哪’。

turns.en: Wait, is Bad Rudy still out or did that get repealed? Bad Rudy still there?
turns.zh: 等等，Bad Rudy 还在吗？还是被撤销了？Bad Rudy 还在吗？

turns.en: And I asked, you know, does Elon know what you say about him? And she goes, 'What is he going to do about it?'
turns.zh: 然后我问，埃隆知道你说他什么吗？它说，‘他能怎么样？’

turns.en: What is he going to do about it?
turns.zh: 他能怎么样？

turns.en: So I just literally took a photo of you and see what it is.
turns.zh: 所以我刚刚真的拍了一张你的照片，看看它怎么说。

turns.en: Did you ask a question?
turns.zh: 你问了问题吗？

turns.en: No, nothing. I didn't say anything.
turns.zh: 没有，什么都没说。

turns.en: This man is hugely... This is Peter Diamandis.
turns.zh: 这个人非常……这是彼得·戴曼迪斯。

turns.en: That's pretty good. There's no context whatsoever.
turns.zh: 很不错。完全没有上下文。

turns.en: The host of the podcast Moonshots. Sometimes that's your first credential now. That's amazing. Forget about everything else I've done in life. Comes back to your podcast. That was a no context image.
turns.zh: 播客《登月计划》的主持人。有时候这现在是你的首要身份了。太棒了。忘掉我一生中做的其他所有事。回到你的播客。那是一张没有上下文的图片。

turns.en: By the way, Grok is awesome. I mean, just phenomenal. I tried to update my Wikipedia page for years, impossibly, and it knows me.
turns.zh: 顺便说一句，Grok 太棒了。我的意思是，简直不可思议。我试图更新我的维基百科页面好几年，根本不可能，而它认识我。

turns.en: He's wearing a black quilted jacket featuring a Sundance logo.
turns.zh: 他穿着一件黑色绗缝夹克，上面有圣丹斯标志。

turns.en: Not quite true. It's my abundance logo, but I guess a little wrinkled. Can it see it?
turns.zh: 不太准确。那是我的富足标志，但可能有点皱了。它能看见吗？

turns.en: I think so.
turns.zh: 我觉得能。

turns.en: But it basically, it's pretty damn good.
turns.zh: 但基本上，它非常棒。

turns.en: He's smiling and relaxed with a laptop in front of him.
turns.zh: 他微笑着，很放松，面前有一台笔记本电脑。

turns.en: That's true. Well, I should say quite a circuit though.
turns.zh: 没错。不过我得说，真是个电路啊。

turns.en: You got to test it on the roast him.
turns.zh: 你得在吐槽他上测试一下。

turns.en: Only it has to be read by you, though.
turns.zh: 不过只能由你读。

turns.en: I mean, I won't read the whole thing, but give me a taste. I can take it.
turns.zh: 我的意思是，我不会读全部，但给我一点尝尝。我能承受。

turns.en: Okay. Check out that grin. Dude smiling like he just discovered a new way to monetize hope.
turns.zh: 好吧。看看那咧嘴笑。这家伙笑得像刚发现了一种新的变现希望的方式。

turns.en: Monetizing hope. Oh, that's...
turns.zh: 变现希望。哦，那是……

ts.sec: Can AI and Tech Save America and the World?

turns.en: I want to try and answer the question, can AI and tech help save America and the world? Right. I want to give people listening a dose of optimism. There's a survey done in mid December by Pew that said 45% of Americans would rather live in the past and only 14% said they'd rather live in the future, which is insane to me, right? Obviously they never read history. The challenge is most Americans all they have of the future. It's like Hollywood has shown us killer AIs and rogue robots, right? And people are worried about their jobs. They're worried about healthcare.
turns.zh: 我想尝试回答这个问题：AI 和科技能帮助拯救美国和世界吗？对。我想给听众一剂乐观主义。皮尤在 12 月中旬做了一项调查，说 45%的美国人宁愿活在过去，只有 14%说他们宁愿活在未来，这对我来说太疯狂了，对吧？显然他们从未读过历史。挑战在于大多数美国人对未来的全部认知就是好莱坞展示的杀人 AI 和 rogue 机器人，对吧？人们担心他们的工作，担心医疗保健。

ts.sec: Optimism about AI and the future

turns.en: They're worried about the cost of living. The challenge is how do we help people? I mean, you posted on X, the future is going to be amazing with AI and robots enabling sustainable abundance.
turns.zh: 他们担心生活成本。挑战在于我们如何帮助人们？我的意思是，你在 X 上发帖说，未来将因 AI 和机器人实现可持续的富足而变得美好。

turns.en: I think of you when I did that.
turns.zh: 我发帖时想到了你。

turns.en: Thank you. I appreciate that.
turns.zh: 谢谢。我很感激。

turns.en: It's like what would Peter do? You want to say?
turns.zh: 就像彼得会怎么做？你想说吗？

turns.en: Yeah, I was channeling you.
turns.zh: 是的，我在模仿你。

turns.en: Thank you. I couldn't agree more. I didn't agree more either.
turns.zh: 谢谢。我完全同意。我也完全同意。

turns.en: That's great. So my question is from a first-principles standpoint, the rationale for optimism. How do we head towards Star Trek and not Terminator? How do we head towards Roddenberry not Cameron?
turns.zh: 太好了。所以我的问题是，从第一性原理的角度看，乐观的理由是什么？我们如何走向《星际迷航》而不是《终结者》？如何走向罗登贝里而不是卡梅隆？

turns.en: Yeah, Jim. The diverging path meme.
turns.zh: 是的，吉姆。分叉路径的梗。

turns.en: Yes, it is. Avatar has some hopeful parts, but anyway. How do we go towards universal high income instead of social unrest?
turns.zh: 是的，确实如此。《阿凡达》有一些充满希望的部分，但不管怎样。我们如何走向全民高收入而不是社会动荡？

turns.en: Both want social unrest.
turns.zh: 两者都想要社会动荡。

turns.en: So have universal high income and social unrest? That's my prediction.
turns.zh: 所以既有全民高收入又有社会动荡？那是我的预测。

turns.en: Oh, that will make for a lot of problems.
turns.zh: 哦，那会带来很多问题。

turns.en: Is that your actual prediction?
turns.zh: 那是你实际的预测吗？

turns.en: Yeah, it seems likely.
turns.zh: 是的，似乎很有可能。

turns.en: Tell me to push back on it.
turns.zh: 让我反驳一下。

turns.en: Yeah, exactly. But it seems like that's the trend.
turns.zh: 是的，没错。但趋势似乎就是这样。

turns.en: Well, because there's going to be so much change.
turns.zh: 嗯，因为会有太多变化。

turns.en: Yeah, people are going to be scared shitless.
turns.zh: 是的，人们会吓得要死。

turns.en: It's like be careful what you wish for because you might get it. If you actually get all the stuff you want, is that actually the future you want? Because it means that your job won't be what matters if you're living an unchallenged life. With no challenges. If you become a couch potato, if it's the Wall-E future, that does not go well for humans.
turns.zh: 这就像小心你的愿望，因为它可能会实现。如果你真的得到了所有你想要的东西，那真的是你想要的未来吗？因为那意味着你的工作不再重要，如果你过着没有挑战的生活。没有挑战。如果你变成一个沙发土豆，如果是《机器人总动员》的未来，那对人类来说并不好。

turns.en: Well, and we're used to being told, here's your challenge.
turns.zh: 嗯，而且我们习惯了被告知，这是你的挑战。

turns.en: So people haven't historically been very good at creating their own challenge in the absence of... I think Elon does a damn good job. Every time one company takes off, you start your next.
turns.zh: 所以历史上人们并不擅长在没有挑战的情况下自己创造挑战。我认为埃隆做得非常好。每次一家公司起飞，你就开始下一家。

turns.en: Oh, that's rare for punishment.
turns.zh: 哦，那真是罕见地自讨苦吃。

turns.en: I think you are. I think you overthank God for that.
turns.zh: 我认为你是。我认为你为此感谢上帝。

turns.en: So why do I do this to myself? Actually, after AI and robots, is there another thing after that? I guess there's conquering the universe.
turns.zh: 所以我为什么这样对自己？实际上，在 AI 和机器人之后，还有别的东西吗？我想还有征服宇宙。

turns.en: Yeah, there is that. Rocks really.
turns.zh: 是的，还有那个。岩石，真的。

turns.en: Well, and energy. Rocks are your friends.
turns.zh: 嗯，还有能源。岩石是你的朋友。

turns.en: Conquering... We didn't even get there. Why, Elon? Why are you so optimistic? Are you optimistic? Let's start there.
turns.zh: 征服……我们甚至还没到那一步。为什么，埃隆？你为什么如此乐观？你乐观吗？让我们从那里开始。

turns.en: I'm not as optimistic as you are.
turns.zh: 我没有你那么乐观。

turns.en: Okay. But why are you optimistic?
turns.zh: 好吧。但你为什么乐观？

turns.en: I'm more optimistic than most people. And is the trend upward compared to a year ago, two years ago? Well, I think if you reframe things in terms of progress bar, like speaking of challenges, progress towards a Kardashev Type II scale civilization. Let's say the aspiration is capturing all the energy from the sun's output. But let's even have a humbler aspiration than that. If we say that our goal is to get a millionth of the sun's energy, that would be more than a thousand times as much energy as could possibly be produced on Earth. So about a half a billionth of the sun's energy reaches Earth. So you'd have to go up three orders of magnitude from that just to get to a millionth. So we're very, very far from even having a billionth of the sun's energy harnessed in any way. So a reasonable goal would be to try to get to a millionth. And if you try to get to a millionth or a thousandth, 0.1%, that's such an enormous... there's not sure what metaphor we'd use here because a hill to climb is not a big enough metaphor.
turns.zh: 我比大多数人乐观。而且趋势相比一年前、两年前是向上的吗？嗯，我认为如果你用进度条来重新定义事物，比如谈到挑战，向卡尔达舍夫 II 型文明规模的进步。假设愿望是捕获太阳输出的所有能量。但让我们甚至有一个更谦逊的愿望。如果我们说我们的目标是获得太阳能量的百万分之一，那将比地球上可能产生的能量多一千倍以上。所以大约只有太阳能量的五十亿分之一到达地球。所以你需要从那个基础上提高三个数量级才能达到百万分之一。所以我们离以任何方式利用太阳能量的十亿分之一还非常非常远。所以一个合理的目标是尝试达到百万分之一。如果你尝试达到百万分之一或千分之一，0.1%，那是如此巨大……不确定这里用什么比喻，因为爬山不是一个足够大的比喻。

turns.en: Gravity well to escape.
turns.zh: 要逃离的重力井。

turns.en: Engineer hell of a gravity well. Exactly. So if you try to get to a millionth of the sun's energy or a thousandth, these are very difficult tasks. And energy is the inner loop for everything right now.
turns.zh: 工程师的一个巨大的重力井。没错。所以如果你尝试获得太阳能量的百万分之一或千分之一，这些都是非常困难的任务。而能源是当前一切的内循环。

turns.en: Yeah. I think the future currency will essentially just be wattage.
turns.zh: 是的。我认为未来的货币本质上就是瓦特数。

turns.en: Yeah. I was thinking is it the ability of a person to control energy and compute, or just energy? I mean the two translate obviously.
turns.zh: 是的。我在想是一个人对能源和算力的控制能力，还是仅仅能源？我的意思是两者显然可以转换。

turns.en: Just like harnessed energy. Like basically how much power is being turned into work of some kind.
turns.zh: 就像被利用的能源。基本上就是有多少能量被转化为某种形式的功。

turns.en: Intelligence or matter manipulation.
turns.zh: 智能或物质操纵。

turns.en: So that's your next big project is going to be energy. You're going to go back to your solar system.
turns.zh: 所以你的下一个大项目将是能源。你要回到你的太阳能系统。

turns.en: You can expand from there and say, what about even getting somewhere on a Kardashev Type III scale, meaning galaxy level. Now you're talking. Now we're back to Star Trek.
turns.zh: 你可以从那里扩展，说，甚至达到卡尔达舍夫 III 型规模，也就是星系级别。这才像话。现在我们回到了《星际迷航》。

turns.en: Expand horizons here. Where there isn't even a horizon because you're not on a planet.
turns.zh: 在这里扩展视野。甚至没有地平线，因为你不在行星上。

turns.en: So think galaxy mind.
turns.zh: 所以要有星系思维。

turns.en: Yeah. Well, listen, we're in 11.5 million square foot, three pentagons right here in this building. I mean, you think in a reasonably large scale. What is the magnitude?
turns.zh: 是的。嗯，听着，我们在这栋楼里有 1150 万平方英尺，三个五边形。我的意思是，你的思考规模相当大。量级是多少？

turns.en: So from a challenge standpoint, the civilizational challenge will be how do you climb the orders of magnitude in energy harnessed.
turns.zh: 所以从挑战的角度看，文明的挑战将是如何在利用的能源上攀登数量级。

turns.en: But we're going back to why are you optimistic right now? I mean, when people think about the challenges ahead, I think we're going to end up with abundance in the long run, beyond abundance in any beyond what people possibly could think of as abundance. Like AI and robots, the limit will saturate all human desire. And then we get to nanotechnology which takes it even a step further.
turns.zh: 但我们回到你此刻为什么乐观？我的意思是，当人们思考未来的挑战时，我认为长期来看我们会实现富足，超出人们可能想象的任何富足。比如 AI 和机器人，极限将满足所有人类欲望。然后我们进入纳米技术，这又向前迈进了一步。

turns.en: The thing about... I'm not sure what you mean by nano. You mean like little nanobots?
turns.zh: 关于……我不确定你说的纳米是什么意思。你是指小纳米机器人吗？

turns.en: Atomic reassembly. For health.
turns.zh: 原子重组。用于健康。

turns.en: Oh yeah. Sure. Sure. I mean we're already doing atomic level assembly on circuits, you know. Two or three nanometers.
turns.zh: 哦，是的。当然。当然。我的意思是，我们已经在电路上做原子级组装了，你知道。两到三纳米。

turns.en: Amazing.
turns.zh: 太神奇了。

turns.en: It's only depending on how they're arrayed four or five silicon atoms per nanometer.
turns.zh: 这取决于它们的排列方式，每纳米大约四到五个硅原子。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: So those are big atoms though. They're not big-ish. I mean, but I'm just saying you could actually describe the circuits in terms of an integer number of atoms in a specific place.
turns.zh: 不过那些原子挺大的。不算特别大，但我是说，你其实可以用特定位置上的整数个原子来描述电路。

turns.en: They should. It's all angstroms now.
turns.zh: 应该这样。现在都用埃（Å）了。

turns.en: You could just say it's like we'll call this the seven atom node, you know, like you say two nanometers, it's like no one knows, nine silicon atoms something like that. They've got silicon and copper, but a bunch of these things are just marketing numbers. Like the two nanometer is just a marketing number.
turns.zh: 你可以直接说这是七原子节点，就像你说两纳米，没人知道具体是什么，大概是九个硅原子之类的。它们有硅和铜，但很多这些只是营销数字。比如两纳米就是个营销数字。

turns.en: Oh yeah.
turns.zh: 哦，是的。

turns.en: But you still need essentially close to atomic level precision. The atoms really need to be in the right spot.
turns.zh: 但本质上你仍然需要接近原子级的精度。原子必须放在正确的位置。

turns.en: So I think they're getting clean rooms wrong by the way in these modern fabs. I'm going to make a bet here. Okay.
turns.zh: 所以我觉得现代晶圆厂的洁净室搞错了。我要打个赌。

turns.en: Okay.
turns.zh: 好。

turns.en: That Tesla will have a 2nm fab and I can eat a cheeseburger and smoke a cigar in the fab.
turns.zh: 特斯拉会建一座两纳米晶圆厂，我可以在里面吃芝士汉堡、抽雪茄。

turns.en: Oh, come on.
turns.zh: 哦，得了吧。

turns.en: Yes. The air handling will be that good.
turns.zh: 是的。空气处理系统会那么好。

turns.en: Do you have this sketched out in your mind? Like how are the atoms being placed that they're immune to cheeseburger grease?
turns.zh: 你脑子里有草图吗？原子怎么放置才能不受芝士汉堡油脂的影响？

turns.en: They just maintain wafer isolation the entire time, which is actually the default for fabs. The wafers are transported in boxes of pure nitrogen gas under a slight positive pressure.
turns.zh: 他们全程保持晶圆隔离，这其实是晶圆厂的默认做法。晶圆在略正压的纯氮气盒中运输。

turns.en: So are the bananas at Walmart, just so you know.
turns.zh: 沃尔玛的香蕉也是，跟你说一下。

turns.en: Yeah. Well, it's essentially like it's pretty hard for anything that's combusting to live without oxygen.
turns.zh: 是啊。本质上，任何燃烧的东西没有氧气都很难存活。

turns.en: Yep.
turns.zh: 对。

turns.en: So you can kill the bugs just by putting a nitrogen blanket on plants.
turns.zh: 所以给植物盖上氮气毯就能杀死虫子。

turns.en: Yeah. Interesting.
turns.zh: 嗯，有意思。

turns.en: I want to talk about energy, health, education because those are people's concerns. So, on the energy front, the innermost loop of everything that you're building and doing right now, energy is the foundation. What's your vision for energy abundance?
turns.zh: 我想谈谈能源、健康和教育，因为这些都是人们关心的问题。在能源方面，你现在所做的一切最核心的循环中，能源是基础。你对能源充裕的愿景是什么？

turns.en: The sun. In the next decade. The sun. Yeah. I mean, so the sun is everything.
turns.zh: 太阳。未来十年。太阳。是的。我的意思是，太阳就是一切。

turns.en: It's everything. So, you're all in on solar.
turns.zh: 它就是一切。所以你全力押注太阳能。

turns.en: I mean, yeah. I mean your natural gas and solar, you're at Colossus 2, right?
turns.zh: 我是说，是的。我是说你的天然气和太阳能，你在巨像二号（Colossus 2），对吧？

turns.en: People just don't understand how solar is everything. So everything compared to the sun, all other energy sources are like cavemen throwing some twigs into a fire.
turns.zh: 人们就是不明白太阳能有多重要。与太阳相比，所有其他能源都像穴居人往火里扔几根树枝。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: So the sun is over 99.8% of all mass in the solar system. Jupiter is around 0.1% of the mass. So even if you burnt Jupiter, the energy produced by the sun would still round up to 100%.
turns.zh: 太阳占太阳系总质量的 99.8%以上。木星约占 0.1%。所以即使你燃烧木星，太阳产生的能量仍然会四舍五入到 100%。

turns.en: Mhm.
turns.zh: 嗯。

turns.en: And then if you teleported three more Jupiters into our solar system and burnt them too, it would still round up. The sun still rounds up to 100% of energy.
turns.zh: 如果你再传送三个木星到太阳系并燃烧它们，仍然会四舍五入。太阳仍然占能量的 100%。

turns.en: Any interest in fusion?
turns.zh: 对聚变有兴趣吗？

turns.en: I mean like fusion on a planet. You know what? You know coming a mile away. You're never going to guess how the sun works. Giant coal plants.
turns.zh: 我是说行星上的聚变。你知道吗？你一眼就能看出来。你永远猜不到太阳是怎么工作的。巨大的燃煤电厂。

turns.en: I mean, we have a giant free fusion reactor that shows up every day 93 million miles away. It's farcical for us to create little fusion reactors. I mean that would be like having a tiny ice cube maker in the Antarctic and say, 'Hey, look, we made ice.' I'm like, 'Congratulations. You're in the Antarctic.'
turns.zh: 我的意思是，我们有一个巨大的免费聚变反应堆，每天在 9300 万英里外出现。我们制造小聚变反应堆很荒谬。那就像在南极洲有一个小制冰机，然后说：‘嘿，看，我们造了冰。’我会说：‘恭喜你。你在南极洲。’

turns.en: So totally with you on this. It's like 3 kilometer high glaciers right next to you.
turns.zh: 完全同意。就像你旁边有 3 公里高的冰川。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: If you just narrow the question to the Memphis timeline. So, Memphis data center timeline between a gigawatt and 10 gig. You're not going to pull 10 gigawatts out of Memphis. Maybe you are two or three.
turns.zh: 如果你把问题缩小到孟菲斯的时间线。孟菲斯数据中心的时间线在 1 吉瓦到 10 吉瓦之间。你不可能从孟菲斯抽出 10 吉瓦。也许你能抽出两三个吉瓦。

turns.en: Two or three. Okay. So there's still a gap between there and the next whatever you just draw. So they're not in space yet at that point.
turns.zh: 两三个。好的。所以那里和你刚才画的之间还有差距。那时它们还没到太空。

turns.en: So we're still in toy land here. For on toy land you toy land. 10 gigawatt.
turns.zh: 所以我们还在玩具世界里。在玩具世界里，10 吉瓦。

turns.en: You know what's amazing is there's 100 megawatts right outside the door here and it's massive. Yeah. It's enormous. And it uses more energy than everything. All these manufacturing lines combined use less energy than that.
turns.zh: 你知道吗，门外就有 100 兆瓦，而且规模巨大。是的。它非常庞大。它消耗的能量比所有东西都多。所有这些生产线加起来消耗的能量都比它少。

turns.en: I think but we're talking about a long go. Cortex one was the third largest training cluster in the world for doing coherent training.
turns.zh: 我想但我们说的是很久以前。Cortex One 是世界上第三大训练集群，用于进行连贯训练。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: You're falling behind.
turns.zh: 你落后了。

turns.en: Well, we have Cortex 2 that's being built out. That'll be half a gigawatt and operational middle of next year.
turns.zh: 嗯，我们正在建设 Cortex 2。那将是 0.5 吉瓦，明年年中投入运营。

turns.en: Hey everybody. You may not know this, but I've got an incredible research team. And every week myself, my research team study the meta trends that are impacting the world. Topics like computation, sensors, networks, AI, robotics, 3D printing, synthetic biology. And these meta trend reports I put out once a week enable you to see the future 10 years ahead of anybody else. If you'd like to get access to the Metatrends newsletter every week, go to dmandis.com/metatrends. That's damandis.com/metatrends. So going back to what Dave is saying over the next five years, what are you scaling on energy front?
turns.zh: 嘿，大家好。你可能不知道，我有一个很棒的研究团队。每周我和我的研究团队都会研究影响世界的宏观趋势。主题包括计算、传感器、网络、人工智能、机器人、3D 打印、合成生物学。我每周发布的这些宏观趋势报告能让你比其他人提前十年看到未来。如果你想每周获取宏观趋势通讯，请访问 dmandis.com/metatrends。那是 damandis.com/metatrends。回到 Dave 刚才说的，未来五年，你在能源方面会扩大什么规模？

turns.en: I mean five years is a long time. I mean energy, I mean China has done an incredible job. Right. It's running circles around us. China has done an incredible job on solar. It's amazing. So I believe China's production capacity is around 1500 gigawatts per year of solar.
turns.zh: 五年是很长的时间。能源方面，中国做得非常出色。没错。它把我们远远甩在后面。中国在太阳能方面做得非常出色。太惊人了。所以我相信中国的太阳能年产能大约在 1500 吉瓦左右。

turns.en: Yeah. They put in 500 terawatt hours in the last year. Terawatt hours. Yeah. 500 terawatt hours to be very specific in the last year. 70% of that was solar and they're just scaling.
turns.zh: 是的。他们去年安装了 500 太瓦时。太瓦时。是的。准确地说，去年是 500 太瓦时。其中 70%是太阳能，而且他们还在扩大规模。

turns.en: Do you imagine that solar scales? Do you imagine that the US could make that level of investment and commitment? I mean because people are worried about their energy bills going up with no data centers in our backyard. How do we provide? I mean energy is equivalent to cost of living. It's equivalent to health. It's equivalent to clean water. The higher energy production of a country, the higher its GDP. Energy is important.
turns.zh: 你想象过太阳能会大规模发展吗？你想象过美国能做出那种水平的投资和承诺吗？因为人们担心能源账单上涨，而我们的后院没有数据中心。我们如何提供？能源相当于生活成本。它相当于健康。它相当于清洁水源。一个国家的能源产量越高，其 GDP 就越高。能源很重要。

turns.en: So what should we do to scale that way? Do we do it in solar here?
turns.zh: 那我们该怎么以那种方式扩展？在地球上搞太阳能吗？

turns.en: Um, I think we should scale solar substantially in the US. Tesla and SpaceX are scaling solar. And I encourage others to do so as well.
turns.zh: 嗯，我认为我们应该在美国大幅扩展太阳能。特斯拉和 SpaceX 正在扩大太阳能规模。我也鼓励其他人这样做。

turns.en: So I've said publicly, I do see a path to 100 gigawatts a year of space solar, sort of AI-powered solar-powered AI satellites.
turns.zh: 所以我公开说过，我确实看到了一条每年 100 吉瓦太空太阳能的路径，有点像 AI 驱动的太阳能 AI 卫星。

turns.en: Yes, 100 gigawatts a year of solar-powered AI satellites. I did the math on that. That's like 500,000 Starlink V3s launched over 8,000 Starship flights. That's one every hour for a year. 10,000 flights a year is a reasonable number.
turns.zh: 是的，每年 100 吉瓦的太阳能 AI 卫星。我算过，那大约需要 50 万颗星链 V3 卫星，通过 8000 次星舰飞行发射。相当于一年每小时一次。每年一万次飞行是个合理的数字。

turns.en: It's amazing. Quite the scale. What's the rough timeline? Because by aircraft standards, that's a small number.
turns.zh: 太惊人了。规模真大。大概的时间线是什么？因为按航空标准，那是个小数字。

turns.en: Sure. In terms of flights, yeah, for sure. It depends what you compare it to. Compared to the rest of the rocket industry, it's a very high number.
turns.zh: 当然。就飞行次数而言，确实。取决于你拿什么比。和火箭行业的其他部分相比，这个数字非常高。

turns.en: And we're talking about a million tons of payload to orbit per year. So if you do a million tons of payload to orbit per year with 100 kilowatts per ton, that's 100 gigawatts of solar-powered AI satellites per year.
turns.zh: 我们说的是每年一百万吨有效载荷入轨。如果每年一百万吨有效载荷，每吨 100 千瓦，那就是每年 100 吉瓦的太阳能 AI 卫星。

turns.en: Yeah. There's a path to get probably to a terawatt per year. If you want to go up another order of magnitude, say 100 terawatts a year, which is obviously kind of nutty numbers, then you want to make those AI satellites on the moon and use a mass driver. The Gerard K. O'Neill approach.
turns.zh: 是的。有一条路径可能达到每年太瓦。如果你想再提高一个数量级，比如每年 100 太瓦，这显然有点疯狂，那么你需要在月球上制造那些 AI 卫星，并使用质量驱动器。杰拉德·K·奥尼尔的方法。

turns.en: Well, like Robert Heinlein's 'The Moon Is a Harsh Mistress'. Pretty much. I love that book.
turns.zh: 嗯，就像罗伯特·海因莱因的《严厉的月亮》。差不多。我喜欢那本书。

turns.en: Yeah. It's a sort of libertarian paradise on the moon. So, on the moon you can just accelerate the satellites to escape velocity, around 2500 meters per second, and there's no atmosphere, so a mass driver works very well.
turns.zh: 是的。那有点像月球上的自由意志主义天堂。在月球上，你可以直接把卫星加速到逃逸速度，大约每秒 2500 米，而且没有大气，所以质量驱动器效果很好。

turns.en: Can I ask about orbital debris? I mean, we're building effectively a Dyson swarm around the Earth. Are you worried about congestion? Sun-sync orbit is going to fill very quickly.
turns.zh: 我能问一下轨道碎片的问题吗？我们实际上是在地球周围建造一个戴森群。你担心过度拥挤吗？太阳同步轨道很快就会填满。

turns.en: You don't have to use sun-sync. There are pros and cons. Payload to orbit drops by like 30% compared to mid-inclination like 70 degrees.
turns.zh: 你不一定非要用太阳同步轨道。有利有弊。与 70 度左右的中倾角相比，有效载荷入轨会下降大约 30%。

turns.en: Do we need an orbital debris X Prize at this point? Some way to get defunct satellites down. Do we pass rules requiring them to de-orbit on their own?
turns.zh: 我们现在需要设立一个轨道碎片 X 奖吗？需要某种方式让报废卫星下来。我们是否通过规则要求它们自行离轨？

turns.en: At the point where you can put a million tons of satellites into orbit, you can also start bringing down satellites. Or at least collect them into a fixed location so they're not all over the place, and then you can reuse them. The resource level will be so high that I believe this will be a solved problem, given the amount of intelligence we're talking about. The intelligence will be quite interested in preserving itself.
turns.zh: 当你能够将一百万吨卫星送入轨道时，你也能开始把卫星带下来。或者至少把它们收集到一个固定位置，这样它们就不会到处乱飞，然后你可以重复使用它们。资源水平会非常高，我相信考虑到我们讨论的智能水平，这将是一个已解决的问题。智能本身会很有兴趣保护自己。

turns.en: Interesting. Good motivation.
turns.zh: 有意思。好动机。

turns.en: The data centers will not be in low Earth orbit, right? They'll be much higher, constantly in the sun, not in the traffic jam.
turns.zh: 数据中心不会在低地球轨道，对吧？它们会高得多，始终在阳光下，不会在交通堵塞中。

turns.en: You can get constant sunlight at around 1200 kilometers on a sun-synchronous orbit. But you could place them in multiple orbits.
turns.zh: 在太阳同步轨道上，大约 1200 公里高度可以获得持续阳光。但你可以把它们放在多个轨道上。

turns.en: If there's an X Prize for cleaning up, clutter is only going to be in low Earth orbit. Below around 700 or 800 kilometers, atmospheric drag will bring it back.
turns.zh: 如果有清理的 X 奖，碎片只会出现在低地球轨道。大约 700 或 800 公里以下，大气阻力会把它带回来。

turns.en: For Starlink, there's a dual benefit of being as low as possible because beams are tighter, less latency, and smaller beams if you're closer to Earth. Starlink V3 will be around 330 to 350 km, which is quite a lot of drag, so it's constantly thrusting.
turns.zh: 对于星链，尽可能低有双重好处，因为波束更窄，延迟更低，而且离地球越近波束越小。星链 V3 将在 330 到 350 公里左右，阻力相当大，所以需要持续推进。

turns.en: I remember when you proposed Starlink and everybody else in the industry was like, 'No way. He's not going to get the spectrum. He's not going to be able to do this.' It's kind of worked.
turns.zh: 我记得你提出星链时，业内其他人都说，‘不可能。他拿不到频谱。他做不到。’结果它成功了。

turns.en: Yeah, the Starlink team have done an incredible job. We've basically rebuilt the internet in space with laser links. There are 9,000 satellites up there right now.
turns.zh: 是的，星链团队做得非常出色。我们基本上用激光链路在太空中重建了互联网。现在有 9000 颗卫星在那里。

turns.en: Do you think the government will be able to handle the licensing of the volume of satellites you want to put up? Will there be pushback? China is going to put up their own constellations. Europe, who knows?
turns.zh: 你认为政府能处理你计划发射的卫星数量的许可问题吗？会有阻力吗？中国会发射自己的星座。欧洲呢？谁知道？

turns.en: They won't. Nothing they're doing has success in the set of possible outcomes.
turns.zh: 他们不会。他们做的任何事情在可能的结果集中都不会成功。

turns.en: I just got back from Rome. I don't want to touch that railing. Successes are on the set of possible outcomes? The chart showing the number of billion-dollar startups in the US versus Europe is crazy.
turns.zh: 我刚从罗马回来。我不想碰那个栏杆。成功在可能的结果集中？那张显示美国与欧洲十亿美元初创公司数量的图表太疯狂了。

turns.en: Yeah. And data centers too.
turns.zh: 是的。数据中心也是。

turns.en: No one was talking about orbital data centers six months ago. Nobody. Then all of a sudden, Sundar is on it. You're out with it. It's the hot new thing. What happened that every company is now talking about orbital data centers?
turns.zh: 六个月前还没人谈论轨道数据中心。没人。然后突然之间，桑达尔在搞这个。你也推出了。成了热门新事物。发生了什么，现在每家公司都在谈论轨道数据中心？

turns.en: I guess it went viral on X.
turns.zh: 我猜它在 X 上火了。

turns.en: It did. Is every company talking about it?
turns.zh: 确实。每家公司都在谈论吗？

turns.en: Oh, yeah. Everybody's got their own orbital data center.
turns.zh: 哦，是的。每家公司都有自己的轨道数据中心。

turns.en: For sure.
turns.zh: 确实。

ts.sec: Starship Launch Costs and Orbital Data Centers

turns.en: And I was suggesting to Peter that you updated the math on launch costs and that it's a tipping point very quickly with the updated math.
turns.zh: 我跟彼得提过，你更新了发射成本的计算，而且根据新数据，很快就会达到一个临界点。

turns.en: But Starship's been the cost for you know, I don't know what you hold $100 per kilogram, $10 per kilogram. What do you have Starship at? It's possible that Elon said that and nobody believed it until now.
turns.zh: 但星舰的成本，你知道，我不知道你认为是每公斤 100 美元还是 10 美元。你觉得星舰能做到多少？可能埃隆以前说过，但直到现在才有人相信。

turns.en: No, you can go back and look at my what even back when it was Twitter uh the my old tweets. I said these things many years ago.
turns.zh: 不，你可以回去看看我以前的推文，甚至是在它还叫 Twitter 的时候。我很多年前就说过这些。

turns.en: 100 bucks or 10 bucks a kilogram.
turns.zh: 每公斤 100 美元或 10 美元。

turns.en: Yeah. And I said this is we're going to do a million tons a year to orbit. And we've got to get the cost down.
turns.zh: 对。我还说过，我们要每年向轨道运送一百万吨。我们必须降低成本。

turns.en: Yeah. Well below $100 a kilogram.
turns.zh: 是的，远低于每公斤 100 美元。

turns.en: So that's going to move the data centers to orbit.
turns.zh: 所以这会把数据中心搬到轨道上。

turns.en: It will. It's they can do you can basically do the math like if you've got a fully reusable rocket.
turns.zh: 会的。你可以算一下，如果你有一枚完全可重复使用的火箭。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 嗯。

turns.en: Which is fully and rapidly reusable like an aircraft. Then this is an incredibly this is a very difficult thing to do obviously. I think it's at the limit of human intelligence to create a fully and rapidly reusable rocket.
turns.zh: 像飞机一样完全且快速可重复使用。这显然是一件极其困难的事。我认为制造完全且快速可重复使用的火箭已经接近人类智慧的极限。
