turns.zh: 过去五年我一直在麻省理工学院协助教授一门课，叫“AI 创业基础”。每年调查学生时，他们创业的意愿都大幅上升。现在高达 80%。每个人都想成为创始团队的一员。我和彼得在麻省理工的时候，大概只有 10%。他们都想读博士。我不记得有任何关于创业的对话，即使在斯坦福也没有。

turns.en: Every time an Iron Man movie came out, it notched up another probably 10% or so, because everybody wanted to be Tony Stark.
turns.zh: 每次《钢铁侠》电影上映，大概就会再提升 10%左右，因为每个人都想成为托尼·斯塔克。

turns.en: I always thought Tony Stark was modeled on Charles Stark Draper and Howard Hughes. But when Robert Downey Jr. wanted to reinvent it, it's modeled on Elon. He came and met with me.
turns.zh: 我一直以为托尼·斯塔克是以查尔斯·斯塔克·德雷珀和霍华德·休斯为原型的。但小罗伯特·唐尼想要重塑这个角色时，是以你为原型的。他来找过我。

turns.en: Yeah, they came to Jon Favreau and Robert. I like the name Grok. I like Jarvis as well. At some point if Grok gets good enough, we're going to call it Encyclopedia Galactica.
turns.zh: 是啊，他们找了乔恩·费儒和罗伯特。我喜欢 Grok 这个名字。我也喜欢 Jarvis。如果 Grok 足够好了，我们打算叫它《银河系百科全书》。

turns.en: Going back to education, what would you do for middle and high school? You just came back from an announcement with President Bukele in El Salvador.
turns.zh: 回到教育话题，你会为中学教育做些什么？你刚从萨尔瓦多和布克尔总统一起发布了一个公告。

turns.en: It was basically to use Grok for education, like personalization. Hopefully not the vulgar version. We would have a kid-friendly version of Grok. AI can be an individualized teacher that is infinitely patient and answers all your questions. You still need to be curious and want to learn. Grok can't make you want to learn, but it can make learning more interesting. You could probably gamify and incentivize it. Kids need to want to learn.
turns.zh: 主要是用 Grok 来做教育，比如个性化。希望不是那个粗俗的版本。我们会有一个适合儿童的 Grok 版本。AI 可以成为一个个性化的老师，无限耐心，回答你所有的问题。你仍然需要保持好奇心和学习的意愿。Grok 不能让你想学习，但它可以让学习变得更有趣。你也许可以把它游戏化并给予激励。孩子们需要想学习。

turns.en: People should think of the brain as a biological computer.
turns.zh: 人们应该把大脑看作一台生物计算机。

turns.en: Yeah, it's a biological neural net with a number of neurons and neural efficiency. You can't tune any arbitrary kid into Einstein. That's not realistic because Einstein had a very good meat computer. You can't just do Shakespeare, Newton, Einstein type of thing unless the meat computer is exceptional.
turns.zh: 是的，它是一个生物神经网络，有一定数量的神经元和神经效率。你不能把任意一个孩子调教成爱因斯坦。那不现实，因为爱因斯坦有一个非常好的肉计算机。你不能随便就培养出莎士比亚、牛顿、爱因斯坦那样的人，除非那个肉计算机是出类拔萃的。

turns.en: When people say we need to solve education in the United States because it's fundamentally broken, I think what's really broken is the old social contract that says do well in high school, get into a good college, get a degree, and then get a job.
turns.zh: 当人们说我们需要解决美国的教育问题，因为它从根本上已经坏了，我认为真正坏掉的是旧的社会契约：在高中表现好，上好大学，拿到学位，然后找到工作。

turns.en: And I don't know that that's going to be valid in the future. We talk about this on the pod a lot, that the career of the future isn't getting a job. It's being an entrepreneur. It's finding a problem and solving it.
turns.zh: 我不知道这在未来是否还适用。我们在播客里经常讨论，未来的职业不是找一份工作，而是成为创业者，是发现问题并解决它。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: Do you agree with that?
turns.zh: 你同意吗？

turns.en: Right now I'd say people should just go to school for the social experience, use more AI. The conventional schooling experience I think could be a lot better. What we're going to do in El Salvador and hopefully other places, have individualized teachers, that's going to be much better. And you could go to a school with a bunch of other kids I guess if you want to hang out with other kids, but you don't need to.
turns.zh: 目前我会说，人们上学只是为了社交体验，多用 AI。传统的学校教育体验我认为可以改进很多。我们在萨尔瓦多以及希望在其他地方要做的，就是提供个性化教师，那会好得多。你可以去学校和一群孩子一起，如果你想和其他孩子玩的话，但没必要。

turns.en: Right.
turns.zh: 对。

turns.en: You could do it on your phone at home. So that's why I say at this point education is a social experience. When I talk to my kids who are in college, they recognize that they can learn just as much independently. In fact, they would learn more in a work situation.
turns.zh: 你可以在家通过手机学习。所以这就是为什么我说现在的教育是一种社交体验。我和我上大学的孩子们聊天时，他们意识到自己独立学习也能学到同样多。事实上，他们在工作环境中会学得更多。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: They're there for the social experience and to be around a bunch of people of their own age. Sort of a coming of age social experience.
turns.zh: 他们去学校是为了社交体验，和一群同龄人在一起。有点像一种成长的社交体验。

turns.en: Sure. Sure. Being on your own, learning how to lead or defend yourself as the case may be.
turns.zh: 当然。当然。独立自主，学习如何领导或保护自己，视情况而定。

turns.en: Well, yeah. I mean, if you join the workforce, from the perspective of a 19-year-old, you're with a bunch of old people. If you're doing engineering with a bunch of middle-aged dudes, it's like, do you really want to do that or do you want to hang out where there's at least some girls your age type of thing.
turns.zh: 嗯，是的。我的意思是，如果你进入职场，从一个 19 岁的视角看，你周围是一群老年人。如果你和一群中年大叔一起做工程，你会想，你真的想那样做吗？还是想和至少有些同龄女孩的地方混在一起？

turns.en: I want to get back to this when we talk about a lot of other choices. Actually, I want to get back as we get to universal high income, but I want to talk about health and longevity one second.
turns.zh: 我想在我们讨论很多其他选择时回到这个话题。实际上，我想在我们谈到全民高收入时再回来，但我想先花点时间谈谈健康和长寿。

turns.en: US is ranked number one in health expenses worldwide and it's ranked 70th in health span, right? We are really 70th. Is that accurate?
turns.zh: 美国在全球医疗支出中排名第一，但在健康寿命上排名第 70，对吧？我们真的是第 70 名。这准确吗？

turns.en: I think it would be better than 70th for health span. Well, whatever. It is like we just get fat or something. We're not the top 10.
turns.zh: 我认为健康寿命排名应该比第 70 好。嗯，随便吧。就好像我们只是变胖了之类的。我们不在前十。

turns.en: Maybe a Zic can help us plan the rankings there. So would you just run around? We need Cupid. But a Zic. Mjaro Cupid.
turns.zh: 也许一个 Zic 能帮我们规划排名。所以你会到处跑吗？我们需要丘比特。但一个 Zic。Mjaro Cupid。

turns.en: But I think that's a big reason. It's like if people get really fat then their health gets bad.
turns.zh: 但我认为这是一个重要原因。如果人们变得很胖，他们的健康就会变差。

turns.en: Yeah. Well, if they don't have any exercise, health gets bad. Or if they have donuts for breakfast every morning. You still doing that?
turns.zh: 是的。嗯，如果他们不锻炼，健康就会变差。或者如果他们每天早上吃甜甜圈。你还那样做吗？

turns.en: No, actually I'm not.
turns.zh: 不，实际上我不吃了。

turns.en: Okay, that's good. That's good.
turns.zh: 好的，那很好。那很好。

turns.en: Well, first of all, I wasn't eating a lot of donut. I was trying to have 0.4 of a donut, which rounds down to zero. So I figured anything below 0.44 of a donut rounds down to zero.
turns.zh: 嗯，首先，我并没有吃很多甜甜圈。我试图只吃 0.4 个甜甜圈，这四舍五入为零。所以我认为任何低于 0.44 个甜甜圈的量都四舍五入为零。

turns.en: So you and I have had a disagreement on longevity. We had a little bit. Yeah. I was saying we should push to get people to 120, 150, and you were saying people shouldn't live that long.
turns.zh: 所以你我在长寿问题上存在分歧。我们有过一点分歧。是的。我当时说我们应该推动人们活到 120、150 岁，而你说人们不应该活那么久。

turns.en: Yeah. So how long do you want? You know, there's some people in the world that have done some bad things. How long do you want them to live?
turns.zh: 是的。那么你想活多久？你知道，世界上有些人做过坏事。你想让他们活多久？

turns.en: Yeah. Well, it's okay. They can get the longevity.
turns.zh: 是的。嗯，没关系。他们也可以长寿。

turns.en: This is a serious question, though. If we do that, a lot of things are going to happen that we don't...
turns.zh: 但这是个严肃的问题。如果我们那样做，很多我们不想看到的事情会发生……

turns.en: Wait a second. You said one thing that was interesting. You said we need people to die so people change their minds.
turns.zh: 等一下。你说过一件有趣的事。你说我们需要人们死去，这样人们才会改变想法。

turns.en: Oh yes, people don't change their minds, they just die.
turns.zh: 哦是的，人们不会改变想法，他们只是死去。

turns.en: But that makes more sense actually. My response to that, Elon, was the head of GM didn't have to die for Tesla to come along. Lockheed and Northrop and Boeing didn't have to go away. In a meritocracy, the better ideas will dominate. So I'm hoping that I can get you back onto the longevity train. There's a lot going on in longevity right now, right?
turns.zh: 但这实际上更有道理。埃隆，我对那个的回应是，通用汽车的负责人不必死，特斯拉就能出现。洛克希德、诺斯罗普和波音不必消失。在精英体制中，更好的想法会占主导地位。所以我希望能把你拉回长寿的轨道。现在长寿领域有很多进展，对吧？

turns.en: Like what?
turns.zh: 比如什么？

turns.en: Well, David Sinclair is about to start his epigenetic reprogramming trials in humans. It's worked in animals and non-human primates. It's going into humans.
turns.zh: 嗯，大卫·辛克莱即将开始他的表观遗传重编程人体试验。它在动物和非人灵长类动物中有效。即将进入人体试验。

turns.en: Is this like a pill or an injection?
turns.zh: 这是像药丸还是注射？

turns.en: Right now, it's an injection of an adeno-associated virus. It's the three Yamanaka factors.
turns.zh: 目前，是注射一种腺相关病毒。是三个山中因子。

turns.en: Okay.
turns.zh: 好的。

turns.en: We've got a $101 million health span X Prize that's working with 730 teams working on reversing the age of your brain, immune system, and muscle by 20 years. By the way, do you know why it's $101 million?
turns.zh: 我们有一个 1.01 亿美元的健康寿命 X 奖，有 730 个团队致力于将你的大脑、免疫系统和肌肉的年龄逆转 20 年。顺便问一下，你知道为什么是 1.01 亿美元吗？

turns.en: No.
turns.zh: 不知道。

turns.en: Because the primary funder, when they found out your Carbon X Prize was $100 million, he wanted to make it bigger. So it's $101 million.
turns.zh: 因为主要资助者，当他们发现你的碳 X 奖是 1 亿美元时，他想让它更大。所以是 1.01 亿美元。

turns.en: Oh, who is it? Chip Wilson from Lululemon?
turns.zh: 哦，是谁？Lululemon 的 Chip Wilson？

turns.en: Oh, okay. And then evolution out of... but Chip said, 'Can we make it bigger?' I said, 'You put an extra million in, we'll make it $101 million.'
turns.zh: 哦，好的。然后进化……但 Chip 说：‘我们能让它更大吗？’我说：‘你多投一百万，我们就把它变成 1.01 亿美元。’

turns.en: Sounds good.
turns.zh: 听起来不错。

turns.en: It's a good story. But then we got folks like Dario Amodei predicting doubling the human lifespan in the next 10 years.
turns.zh: 这是个好故事。但后来我们听到像 Dario Amodei 这样的人预测未来 10 年人类寿命会翻倍。

turns.en: That's probably correct. I don't know about doubling, but a significant increase.
turns.zh: 那可能是对的。我不知道翻倍，但显著增加。

turns.en: Significant increase. Sure. Which is easily escape velocity. I mean because when... Depending how old you are. Oh yeah, for sure. Or effective age. Yeah.
turns.zh: 显著增加。当然。这很容易达到逃逸速度。我的意思是，因为……取决于你多大。哦是的，当然。或者有效年龄。是的。

turns.en: Yeah. So I think that for... too much and turn into a baby or something.
turns.zh: 是的。所以我认为……太多就会变成婴儿之类的。

turns.en: That's what I'm telling all the students there. It's like 'Peter, what happened?'
turns.zh: 这就是我告诉所有学生的话。就像‘彼得，发生了什么？’

turns.en: Yes. Yes. There is a frozen...
turns.zh: 是的。是的。有一个冷冻的……

turns.en: You got a zero wrong in the dosage. Just a small factor of 10.
turns.zh: 你在剂量上搞错了一个零。只是一个小数点差 10 倍。

turns.en: Grow out of it. It'll be fine. Exactly. You won't remember it.
turns.zh: 会恢复的。没事的。没错。你不会记得的。

turns.en: I mean, wouldn't it be funny if we do this in like 10 years? Okay, we should do it. I'll do it. We'll do it in 10 years for sure. And let's see if we look younger.
turns.zh: 我的意思是，如果我们 10 年后做这个，不是很有趣吗？好的，我们应该做。我会做的。我们 10 年后肯定做。看看我们是否看起来更年轻。

turns.en: That's a good side bet.
turns.zh: 那是个不错的附加赌注。

turns.en: My comment was always, Elon back then was like, you know, late 40s. Wait till he gets into his 60s, he's going to want longevity anymore.
turns.zh: 我的评论一直是，埃隆当时是，你知道，快 50 岁了。等他到了 60 多岁，他就会想要长寿了。

turns.en: I mean, I want things to not hurt.
turns.zh: 我的意思是，我希望身体不疼。

turns.en: Yeah, sure. Of course.
turns.zh: 是的，当然。当然。

turns.en: It seems like it's only a matter of time before you get back pain. Like it's a when, not an if, your back hurts.
turns.zh: 似乎你出现背痛只是时间问题。就像你的背痛是何时发生，而不是是否发生。

turns.en: Arthritis. Yes.
turns.zh: 关节炎。是的。

turns.en: Yeah. Like these things suck basically.
turns.zh: 是的。基本上这些事很糟糕。

turns.en: Being able to sleep through the night without going to the bathroom a lot. It's very much that one.
turns.zh: 能一觉睡到天亮，不用频繁起夜。就是那个。

turns.en: Yeah, it's more than hope. That one. Oh man, that would be like the infinite money one.
turns.zh: 对，那不只是希望。就是那个。哦，那就像无限金钱一样。

turns.en: Why did you invest in longevity? So I can sleep through the night and not go to the bathroom.
turns.zh: 你为什么投资长寿？这样我就能一觉睡到天亮，不用起夜。

turns.en: Bladder, bladder. Yeah. Duration.
turns.zh: 膀胱，膀胱。对，时长。

turns.en: I mean, admittedly, if you have to wear adult diapers, that's a bummer. That's not good. Adult diapers is a real sign that a country is not on the right path when the adult diapers exceed the baby diapers.
turns.zh: 我是说，诚然，如果你得穿成人纸尿裤，那真是糟透了。那不好。成人纸尿裤是一个国家走错路的真正标志，当成人纸尿裤超过婴儿纸尿裤时。

turns.en: Yeah, we're there. South Korea will be there anymore. They already passed that point. They passed that point many years ago. Japan passed the point many years ago.
turns.zh: 是的，我们已经到了。韩国也会这样。他们已经过了那个点。他们很多年前就过了那个点。日本很多年前就过了那个点。

turns.en: Doesn't go well looking at the Japanese economy. No, I mean like South Korea is like one third replacement rate. Crazy.
turns.zh: 看看日本经济，不太妙。不，我是说韩国生育率只有三分之一。疯了。

turns.en: Yeah. So, three generations they're going to be 1/27th. So, 3% of their current size. I mean, North Korea won't need to invade. They can just walk across.
turns.zh: 对。所以，三代之后他们会变成现在的 1/27。也就是当前规模的 3%。我是说，朝鲜都不需要入侵了。他们直接走过来就行。

turns.en: Yeah. This is going to be some people in walkers or something like that. But you've been very verbal about the not overpopulation but massive underpopulation.
turns.zh: 对。到时候会有些拄着助行器的人之类的。但你一直直言不讳地谈论不是人口过剩，而是严重的人口不足。

turns.en: Yeah, I've been saying this for ages.
turns.zh: 对，我说了很多年了。

turns.en: Longevity is going to be an important part of that solution. I also think by the way if you increased the productive life of most Americans by just a few years you'd flip the entire economics here.
turns.zh: 长寿将是这个解决方案的重要部分。另外，我认为如果你把大多数美国人的生产寿命延长几年，就能彻底改变这里的经济。

turns.en: Well, if AI and robots are going to make everything scarce-free basically.
turns.zh: 嗯，如果 AI 和机器人基本上让一切变得不再稀缺。

turns.en: Yeah. But how long would you want to live?
turns.zh: 对。但你想活多久？

turns.en: I want to go to other planetary systems. I want to go and explore the universe. Yeah. I mean, I would like to double my lifespan for sure. I'm not sure I want to talk about immortality, but at least 120 to 150. It's a long time.
turns.zh: 我想去其他行星系统。我想去探索宇宙。对。我是说，我当然想延长一倍寿命。我不确定要不要谈永生，但至少 120 到 150 岁。那是很长的时间。

turns.en: One of the worst curses possible would be 'May you live forever.'
turns.zh: 最糟糕的诅咒之一可能是‘愿你永生’。

turns.en: Yes. That would be one of the worst curses you could possibly give anyone.
turns.zh: 对。那可能是你能给任何人最糟糕的诅咒之一。

turns.en: But I think life's going to get very interesting. Far more. We're going to speedrun Star Trek as my partner Alex Weer Gross says.
turns.zh: 但我认为生活会变得非常有趣。远远更有趣。正如我的合伙人 Alex Weer Gross 所说，我们要速通《星际迷航》。

turns.en: Yeah. Speedrunning Star Trek would be cool.
turns.zh: 对。速通《星际迷航》会很酷。

turns.en: Well, at a minimum your kids will have infinite life expectancy. If you're talking about escape velocity, if you can double lifespan, you're clearly past longevity escape velocity. The idea of 50 years of AI improvement.
turns.zh: 嗯，至少你的孩子会有无限预期寿命。如果你在谈论逃逸速度，如果你能延长一倍寿命，你显然已经过了长寿逃逸速度。50 年 AI 进步的概念。

turns.en: Yeah, it's great. I mean, we're going to have 20 years on this. I don't know. I got too many fish to fry.
turns.zh: 对，很棒。我是说，我们还有 20 年时间。我不知道。我事情太多了。

turns.en: This is something that I think is very obviously solvable. I've long thought that longevity or semi-mortality is an extremely solvable problem. I don't think it's a particularly hard problem. When you consider the fact that your body is extremely synchronized in its age, the clock must be incredibly obvious. Nobody has an old left arm and a young right arm, right? What's keeping them all in sync? You're programmed to die. And so if you change the program, you will live longer.
turns.zh: 我认为这显然是可以解决的。我长期以来一直认为长寿或半永生是一个极其可解的问题。我不认为它特别难。当你考虑到你的身体在年龄上极度同步，时钟一定非常明显。没有人有一只老左臂和一只年轻右臂，对吧？是什么让它们保持同步？你被编程要死亡。所以如果你改变程序，你就会活得更久。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 对。

turns.en: And we've got species like the bowhead whale that can live for 200 years. The Greenland shark can live for 500 years. When I learned that, I said, why can't they? Why can't we? And I said, it's either a hardware problem or software problem, and we're going to have the tech to solve that. I do believe it's this next decade. So the important thing is not to die from something stupid before the solutions come.
turns.zh: 而且我们有像弓头鲸这样的物种能活 200 年。格陵兰鲨能活 500 年。当我了解到这一点时，我说，为什么它们能？为什么我们不能？我说，要么是硬件问题，要么是软件问题，我们会有技术来解决。我确实相信就在未来十年。所以重要的是在解决方案到来之前不要死于愚蠢的事情。

turns.en: In retrospect, the solution to longevity will seem obvious. Extremely obvious.
turns.zh: 事后看来，长寿的解决方案会显得显而易见。极其明显。

turns.en: I think the thing worth working on is exactly what you said. If old ideas don't calcified, old ideas don't just die off, add that to the pile of things we need to think about today because there are a whole host of other AI related things we need to think about today.
turns.zh: 我认为值得努力的事情正是你所说的。如果旧观念不僵化，旧观念不会自动消亡，把它加到我们今天需要考虑的事情堆里，因为今天还有很多其他 AI 相关的事情需要考虑。

turns.en: Let me finish on the longevity point one second. Elon, I want to invite you again. There's a company called Fountain Life that I created with Tony Robbins, Bob Hariri, Bill Kapp, and we do a 200 gigabyte upload of you. Everything knowable about you. Full genome, full imaging, everything. President Biden and the first lady came through, called it an amazing 10 out of 10 experience. I think I don't want you to pull a Steve Jobs and kick the bucket because of something they didn't know. I mean, if you ask yourself, do you actually know what's going on inside your body right now? I did an MRI recently and submitted it to Grok and none of the doctors nor Grok found anything wrong, but that's a fraction of the information, right? It's your full genome, your microbiome, your metabolism, everything. And it's possible. So, don't call me.
turns.zh: 让我在长寿点上说完。埃隆，我想再次邀请你。有一家叫 Fountain Life 的公司，我和托尼·罗宾斯、鲍勃·哈里里、比尔·卡普一起创立的，我们对你进行 200GB 的上传。关于你的一切可知信息。全基因组、全部影像、所有东西。拜登总统和第一夫人来过，称之为令人惊叹的 10 分体验。我认为我不想让你像史蒂夫·乔布斯那样因为一些他们不知道的事情而挂掉。我是说，如果你问问自己，你真的知道你体内现在发生了什么吗？我最近做了个核磁共振，提交给 Grok，没有一个医生或 Grok 发现任何问题，但那只是信息的一小部分，对吧？那是你的全基因组、微生物组、新陈代谢，一切。而且这是可能的。所以，别给我打电话。

turns.en: What's that? Don't call me, bro. We have a center in your water bottle.
turns.zh: 什么？别给我打电话，兄弟。我们在你的水瓶里有个中心。

turns.en: We have... God damn it. Too late. Sorry. It's already in the works.
turns.zh: 我们有……该死的。太晚了。抱歉。已经在进行了。

turns.en: So, can you go through the rationale of UHI? How does universal high income work?
turns.zh: 那么，你能解释一下 UHI 的原理吗？全民高收入是如何运作的？

turns.en: Okay. So there's going to be more digital intelligence than all human intelligence combined, and more humanoid robots than all humans. Assuming we're in a benign scenario, Star Trek, sort of Roddenberry, not Cameron situation.
turns.zh: 好的。所以将会有比所有人类智能总和还多的数字智能，以及比所有人类还多的人形机器人。假设我们处于一个良性的情景，星际迷航式的，有点像罗登贝里，而不是卡梅隆的情况。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 对。

turns.en: Poor Jim.
turns.zh: 可怜的吉姆。

turns.en: Yeah. I mean, I guess it's important to have these sort of counterpoints.
turns.zh: 对。我是说，我想有这些对比很重要。

turns.en: Yeah. Let's not go in that direction. So the robots are going to just do whatever you want. All the blue-collar labor is being done by robots. All data centers are being built by robots.
turns.zh: 对。我们别往那个方向走。所以机器人会做任何你想做的事。所有蓝领劳动都由机器人完成。所有数据中心都由机器人建造。

ts.sec: White-collar jobs first to be replaced

turns.en: The white-collar labor will be the first to go because until you can move atoms, the thing that can be replaced first is anything that involves just digital. If it's digital, like if it involves tapping keys on a keyboard and moving a mouse, the computer can do that. They can do that for sure. You need the humanoid robots to shape atoms. So if all you're doing is changing bits of information, which is white-collar work, that is the first thing that goes.
turns.zh: 白领工作将最先被取代，因为在你能够移动原子之前，最先能被替代的是那些只涉及数字的工作。如果是数字化的，比如敲键盘、动鼠标，电脑就能做。它们肯定能做到。你需要人形机器人来塑造原子。所以如果你做的只是改变信息比特，也就是白领工作，那这就是最先被淘汰的。

turns.en: This is the inspirational part of the podcast, by the way. When is all white-collar work gone? By when?
turns.zh: 顺便说一句，这是播客里鼓舞人心的部分。所有白领工作什么时候消失？到什么时候？

turns.en: Well, there's a lot of inertia. So even with AI at its current state, I'd say you're pretty close to being able to replace half of all jobs. And you know, white-collar jobs include anything like education too. So anything that involves information and anything short of shaping atoms, AI can do probably half or more of those jobs right now. But there's a lot of inertia. People just keep doing the same thing for quite some time. And there actually has to be a company that makes more use of AI that competes with a company that makes less use of AI, creating a forcing function for increased use of AI. Otherwise, the company that still has humans do things that AI can do will still continue to exist.
turns.zh: 嗯，惯性很大。所以即使 AI 处于当前状态，我认为它已经接近能够取代一半的工作了。而且你知道，白领工作也包括教育之类的。所以任何涉及信息、任何不涉及塑造原子的工作，AI 现在可能已经能完成一半以上了。但惯性很大。人们会在相当长一段时间内继续做同样的事情。而且必须有一家更充分利用 AI 的公司与一家较少利用 AI 的公司竞争，从而形成推动 AI 使用的强制力。否则，那些仍然让人类做 AI 能做的事情的公司还会继续存在。

turns.en: Sure.
turns.zh: 当然。

turns.en: Being a computer used to be a job. So it used to be that a human computer was a job. You would compute numbers. It didn't used to be a machine; it used to be a job description. And you can look online, there are these pictures of skyscrapers full of women copying from ledger to ledger, and men too, but mostly women. There were just buildings full of people at desks doing calculations. So they'd be calculating the interest in your bank account or some science experiment or something. But if you wanted calculations done, people would do it. So now one laptop with a spreadsheet can outperform a skyscraper of several hundred human computers doing calculations. Now, if even a few cells in that spreadsheet were done manually, you would not be able to compete with a spreadsheet that was entirely a computer. What this means is that companies that are entirely AI will demolish companies that are not. It won't be a contest.
turns.zh: 以前，“计算机”是一种工作。所以过去人类“计算机”是一份工作。你计算数字。它以前不是机器，而是一个职位描述。你可以在网上看到照片，摩天大楼里满是女性——主要是女性——在账本之间抄写，也有男性。整栋楼里都是坐在桌前做计算的人。他们会计算你银行账户的利息，或者某个科学实验的数据。如果你需要计算，人们会做。现在，一台装有电子表格的笔记本电脑就能胜过一栋摩天大楼里几百个人类“计算机”。现在，如果那个电子表格里哪怕有几个单元格是手动计算的，你就无法与完全由计算机完成的电子表格竞争。这意味着，完全由 AI 驱动的公司将碾压那些不是的公司。这根本不是一个级别的竞争。

turns.en: Right. Agreed. And that flipped.
turns.zh: 对。同意。而且情况反转了。

turns.en: Yeah. Would you want even one cell in your spreadsheet to be manually calculated? That would be the most annoying cell, and you'd be like 'God damn it,' and it gets it wrong a bunch of the time, error rate. So this flipping...
turns.zh: 是啊。你愿意你的电子表格里哪怕有一个单元格是手动计算的吗？那会是最烦人的单元格，你会想‘该死的’，而且它经常出错，错误率很高。所以这种反转……

turns.en: Are we monetizing hope effectively?
turns.zh: 我们是在有效地将希望变现吗？

turns.en: Not at this moment. I think we're at peak doom for people worried about the future of their jobs.
turns.zh: 目前不是。我认为对于担心工作未来的人来说，我们正处于悲观情绪的顶峰。

turns.en: Monetize. We're at peak doom. We're going to do that as a t-shirt and the mug.
turns.zh: 变现。我们正处于悲观顶峰。我们要把这个做成 T 恤和杯子。

turns.en: And the mug.
turns.zh: 还有杯子。

turns.en: Yes. The mug. So, but you have a solution to this, which is UHI.
turns.zh: 对。杯子。那么，你有一个解决方案，就是 UHI。

turns.en: Yes. Everyone can have whatever they want.
turns.zh: 是的。每个人都可以拥有他们想要的一切。

turns.en: So how does that work? How does UHI work?
turns.zh: 那么这怎么运作？UHI 怎么运作？

turns.en: It's a good question. We have to figure out some... I mean, it's not a bumpy road. My concern isn't the long run; it's the next 3 to 7 years. The transition will be bumpy because humans don't like change. Simultaneously, we'll have radical change, social unrest, and immense prosperity. And you can buy all the Cybertrucks you want. Things are going to get very cheap. So this is actually... and frankly, if this doesn't happen, we'd go bankrupt as a country. The national debt is enormous. The interest on the national debt exceeds not just the military budget, but the military budget plus Medicare or Medicaid, one of the two. It's like one trillion in interest, which is growing. And the deficit is growing. So if we don't have AI and robots, we're all going to go bankrupt and we're headed for economic doom. Also, competitive pressure from China. So this is definitely going to happen.
turns.zh: 这是个好问题。我们得想出一些办法……我的意思是，这条路并不平坦。我担心的不是长期，而是未来 3 到 7 年。转型会很坎坷，因为人类不喜欢变化。与此同时，我们将面临剧烈变革、社会动荡和巨大的繁荣。你可以买所有你想要的 Cybertruck。东西会变得非常便宜。所以实际上……坦率地说，如果这不发生，我们国家就会破产。国家债务巨大。国债利息不仅超过了军费预算，而且超过了军费预算加上医疗保险或医疗补助之一。大约是一万亿美元的利息，而且还在增长。赤字也在增长。所以如果我们没有 AI 和机器人，我们都会破产，走向经济末日。还有来自中国的竞争压力。所以这肯定会发生。

turns.en: We're going back to the theme of this talk. How can AI and exponential tech save America and the world? Don't you think that? But I want to hit this because I was quite pessimistic about it and ultimately I decided to be fatalistic and look on the bright side.
turns.zh: 我们回到这次谈话的主题。AI 和指数级技术如何拯救美国和世界？你不这么认为吗？但我想强调这一点，因为我对此相当悲观，最终我决定听天由命，往好的方面看。

turns.en: I've got to see you look on the bright side of life.
turns.zh: 我得看到你看到生活光明的一面。

turns.en: You're sitting there crucified right side.
turns.zh: 你坐在那里，被钉在右边。

turns.en: But this is not about taxation and redistribution.
turns.zh: 但这与税收和再分配无关。

turns.en: Yeah. No, it's... So, how does it work? Reason through it with me.
turns.zh: 对。不，它是……那么，它怎么运作？跟我一起推理一下。

turns.en: Listen, by the way, I'm open to ideas here.
turns.zh: 听着，顺便说一句，我对各种想法持开放态度。

turns.en: Okay. So I'm wondering if instead of universal high income, if it's universal high stuff and services. The UHSS.
turns.zh: 好的。所以我在想，与其说是全民高收入，不如说是全民高物质和服务。UHSS。

turns.en: Yes.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: I guess this is my guess for how things roll out. And by the way, I'm this is going to be a bumpy ride and it's not like I know the answers here. But I have decided to look on the bright side. And I'd like to thank you guys for being an inspiration in this regard.
turns.zh: 我想这是我对事情如何展开的猜测。顺便说一句，这将是一段坎坷的旅程，我并不是说我知道答案。但我决定往好的方面看。我想感谢你们在这方面给我的启发。

turns.en: Thank you. Happy to help. Yeah, because I actually think it's better to be an optimist and wrong than a pessimist and right, for quality of life.
turns.zh: 谢谢。很高兴能帮上忙。是的，因为我实际上认为，就生活质量而言，做一个乐观主义者而犯错，比做一个悲观主义者而正确要好。

turns.en: Yes, for sure. And by the way, there's also not a force of nature. It's under... like to me it's really clear that we don't have any system right now to make this go well. But AI is a critical part of making it go well. And at some point, Grok is going to be addressing this exact topic that we're talking about, or it has to be one of the big four AI machines. I mean, it's coming, dealing with it. There's no velocity knob, right? There's no on-off switch. It is coming and accelerating.
turns.zh: 是的，当然。顺便说一句，这也不是自然的力量。它处于……对我来说很明显，我们现在没有任何系统能让这件事顺利进行。但 AI 是让它顺利进行的关键部分。在某个时候，Grok 将会讨论我们正在谈论的这个确切话题，或者它必须是四大 AI 机器之一。我的意思是，它正在到来，我们必须应对。没有速度旋钮，对吧？没有开关。它正在到来，并且加速。

ts.sec: AI and robotics as a supersonic tsunami

turns.en: I call AI and robotics the supersonic tsunami.
turns.zh: 我把人工智能和机器人称为超音速海啸。

turns.en: Yes.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: Which maybe is a little alarming.
turns.zh: 这可能有点令人担忧。

turns.en: You think it's good. That's good. Well, because the wake up call.
turns.zh: 你觉得这是好事。那很好。因为这是一个警钟。

turns.en: This is important for folks to grok because I don't want to leave people depressed. I want people to understand what's coming. So we're basically demonetizing everything. I mean labor becomes the cost of capex and electricity. AI is basically intelligence available at a diminimus price. So you're able to produce almost anything. Things get down to basic cost of materials and electricity, right? So people can have whatever stuff they want, whatever services they need.
turns.zh: 这一点很重要，大家要理解，因为我不想让人们沮丧。我想让人们知道即将发生什么。所以我们基本上是在让一切失去货币价值。我的意思是，劳动力变成了资本支出和电力的成本。人工智能基本上是以极低价格提供的智能。所以你几乎可以生产任何东西。成本降到只有原材料和电力，对吧？所以人们可以拥有他们想要的任何东西，任何他们需要的服务。

turns.en: It's not when we say universal high income, it sounds like it's a tax and redistribute, but that's not the case.
turns.zh: 当我们说普遍高收入时，听起来像是征税和再分配，但事实并非如此。

turns.en: It's my best guess for how this will manifest is that prices will drop.
turns.zh: 我最好的猜测是，这将表现为价格下降。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: So as the efficiency of production or the provision of services drops, prices will drop. I mean prices in dollar terms are the ratio between the output of goods and services and the money supply.
turns.zh: 所以随着生产效率或服务提供效率的提高，价格会下降。我的意思是，以美元计价的价格是商品和服务产出与货币供应量的比率。

turns.en: Sure. So if your output of goods and services increases faster than the money supply, you will have deflation and or vice versa.
turns.zh: 当然。所以如果你的商品和服务产出增长快于货币供应量，就会出现通缩，反之亦然。

turns.en: It's a good thing we're growing the money supply so quickly then, right?
turns.zh: 那我们这么快地增加货币供应量是好事，对吧？

turns.en: I yes. That's why I came like let's not worry about growing the money supply. It won't matter because the output of goods and services actually will grow faster than the money supply. And I think we'll be in this and this is a prediction I think some others have made but I will add to it which is that I think governments will actually be pushing to increase money supply faster.
turns.zh: 是的。这就是为什么我说不用担心货币供应增长。这没关系，因为商品和服务的产出实际上会比货币供应增长更快。我认为我们会处于这种状态，这是一个我认为其他人也做过的预测，但我要补充一点，我认为政府实际上会推动更快地增加货币供应。

turns.en: Yes. They won't be able to waste the money fast enough which is saying something.
turns.zh: 是的。他们浪费钱的速度都赶不上，这很说明问题。

turns.en: Isn't it crazy how close those timelines just randomly worked out? I mean at the rate because we're expanding the national debt not because we're anticipating AI. We were going to do that no matter what.
turns.zh: 这些时间线如此巧合地接近，是不是很疯狂？我的意思是，按照这个速度，我们增加国债并不是因为预见到了人工智能。我们无论如何都会这么做。

turns.en: And so it's like right on the edge of becoming Argentina.
turns.zh: 所以就像快要变成阿根廷一样。

turns.en: But yeah, at the time so productivity is going to improve dramatically and it is improving dramatically. I think we'll see high double digit output of goods and services. We have to be a little careful about how economists measure things.
turns.zh: 但是的，届时生产率将大幅提高，而且已经在大幅提高。我认为我们会看到商品和服务产出实现两位数的增长。我们得小心经济学家如何衡量这些东西。

turns.en: There's my favorite joke. I have a few economist jokes but maybe my favorite one is: two economists are going for a walk in the forest and they come across a pile of shit. One economist says 'I'll pay you 100 bucks to eat a pile of shit.' I've heard this one. This is great. Go ahead.
turns.zh: 我最喜欢的一个笑话是：两个经济学家在森林里散步，遇到一堆屎。一个经济学家说：‘我给你 100 美元，你把这堆屎吃了。’我听过这个。太棒了。继续。

turns.en: And so the guy takes 100 bucks and eats the shit. Then they keep walking. They come across another pile of shit. And the other guy says, 'Okay, I'll give you a hundred bucks to eat a pile of shit.' So he gives him a hundred bucks and then the guys say, 'Wait a second. We both have the same amount of money. We both ate a pile of shit.'
turns.zh: 于是那个人拿了 100 美元吃了屎。然后他们继续走。又遇到一堆屎。另一个人说：‘好，我给你 100 美元，你把这堆屎吃了。’于是他给了他 100 美元，然后两人说：‘等等，我们俩钱一样多，都吃了一堆屎。’

turns.en: Oh my god. It sounds like...
turns.zh: 哦，天哪。听起来像……

turns.en: But we increased the economy by $200.
turns.zh: 但我们让经济增加了 200 美元。

turns.en: This is the kind of thing you get in economics. So if you say just the output of goods and services, it will be much greater. You just need...
turns.zh: 这就是经济学里会出现的情况。所以如果你只说商品和服务的产出，它会大得多。你只需要……

turns.en: So profitability of companies goes through the roof at some point.
turns.zh: 所以公司的盈利能力在某个时候会飙升。

ts.sec: Taxation and redistribution in an AI-driven economy

turns.en: But the question becomes: is that taxed by the government and redistributed as some level of income as a UHI or UBI? In other words, one of the questions is if in fact this future we hit massive productivity and massive profitability because we're dividing by zero. The cost of labor has gone to nothing. The cost of intelligence has gone to nothing and we're still producing products and services faster and faster. So there's more profitability. Someone needs to be buying it and someone needs to be able to have the capital to buy it. This is an important question to get thought through.
turns.zh: 但问题来了：政府会不会对此征税，然后以某种水平的收入（如普遍高收入或全民基本收入）进行再分配？换句话说，问题之一是，如果我们真的迎来了这个未来，实现了巨大的生产率和巨大的盈利能力，因为我们在除以零。劳动力成本降为零，智能成本降为零，而我们仍在以越来越快的速度生产产品和服务。所以盈利能力更强了。但需要有人来购买，需要有人有资本来购买。这是一个需要深思的重要问题。

turns.en: Yeah. One side recommendation I have is: don't worry about squirreling money away for retirement in like 10 or 20 years. It won't matter.
turns.zh: 是的。我有一个附带建议：别担心为 10 年或 20 年后的退休存钱。那不重要。

turns.en: No.
turns.zh: 不。

turns.en: Either we're not going to be here or it just won't matter. You won't need to save for retirement. If any of the things that we've said are true, saving for retirement will be irrelevant. The services will be there to support you. You'll have the home, you'll have the healthcare, you'll have the entertainment.
turns.zh: 要么我们不会在这里，要么这根本不重要。你不需要为退休储蓄。如果我们说的任何一件事是真的，那么为退休储蓄就无关紧要了。服务会支持你。你会有房子、医疗保健、娱乐。

turns.en: The way this unfolds is fundamentally impossible to predict because of self-improvement of the AI and the accelerating timeline.
turns.zh: 由于人工智能的自我改进和加速的时间线，这一切如何展开从根本上说是无法预测的。

turns.en: Yeah. It's called singularity for a reason.
turns.zh: 是的。它被称为奇点是有原因的。

turns.en: Yeah. Exactly.
turns.zh: 是的。没错。

turns.en: I don't know what happens after the event horizon.
turns.zh: 我不知道事件视界之后会发生什么。

turns.en: Exactly. You can never see past the black hole or the event horizon. The light cone.
turns.zh: 没错。你永远看不到黑洞或事件视界之外。光锥。

turns.en: I mean Ray has a singularity out way too far. I mean this is like the next... what's your timeline for this?
turns.zh: 我的意思是，雷把奇点设得太远了。这就像下一个……你对这个的时间线是什么？

turns.en: We're in the singularity.
turns.zh: 我们就在奇点中。

turns.en: Well, we are in the singularity for sure. We're in the midst of it right now for sure.
turns.zh: 嗯，我们肯定在奇点中。我们现在肯定就在其中。

turns.en: And we're in this beautiful sweet spot which is... we're the roller coaster's just...
turns.zh: 我们正处于这个美妙的甜蜜点……我们就像过山车刚刚……

turns.en: Yeah. Exactly. That's a great analogy. It's like that feeling.
turns.zh: 是的。没错。这个比喻很好。就是那种感觉。

turns.en: You're at the top of the roller coaster and you're about to go.
turns.zh: 你就在过山车的顶端，马上就要冲下去了。

turns.en: Yeah. But you know it's going to be a lot of G's when you hit it.
turns.zh: 是的。但你知道当你冲下去时会承受很大的重力。

turns.en: And it's like people like I don't have to just have courtside seats. I'm on the court.
turns.zh: 就像人们说，我不只是有场边座位，我就在球场上。

turns.en: Exactly.
turns.zh: 没错。

turns.en: And it blows my mind still sometimes multiple times a week.
turns.zh: 而且它仍然让我震惊，有时一周好几次。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: And so just when I think I'm like wow. And then it's like two days later more wow.
turns.zh: 所以每当我以为‘哇’的时候，两天后又有更‘哇’的事情。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: Exponential wow. Yeah, I think we'll hit AGI next year in 26.
turns.zh: 指数级的‘哇’。是的，我认为我们将在明年 2026 年实现 AGI。

turns.en: Yeah, I heard you say that.
turns.zh: 是的，我听到你这么说。

turns.en: Yeah, I've said that for a while actually.
turns.zh: 是的，实际上我已经说了一段时间了。

turns.en: And then you said by 2029 2030 equivalent to the entire human race.
turns.zh: 然后你说到 2029 年、2030 年，相当于整个人类。

turns.en: 2030 we exceed like I'm confident by then AI will exceed the intelligence of all humans combined. That's way pessimistic if you hit AGI next year and that date is in flux but from that date to self-improvements that are on the order of a thousand or 10,000x just algorithmic improvements is very short.
turns.zh: 2030 年我们会超越，我确信到那时人工智能将超越所有人类智能的总和。如果你明年就实现 AGI，那这个日期就太悲观了，而且这个日期是变动的，但从那个日期到自我改进，仅仅是算法改进就能达到 1000 倍或 10000 倍，时间非常短。

turns.en: So everybody why isn't everybody talking about this right now?
turns.zh: 那么为什么现在不是每个人都在谈论这个？

turns.en: Well I mean on X they are.
turns.zh: 嗯，我的意思是，在 X 上他们在谈论。

turns.en: Yes.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: I'll tell you something that most people in the AI community don't yet understand. Almost no one understands this. The intelligence density potential is vastly greater than what we're currently experiencing. I think we're off by two orders of magnitude in terms of the intelligence density per gigabyte, per gigawatt of energy, per file size. If you have a say, get intelligence on your drives on your laptop, parameters, the same thing. Two orders of magnitude improvement from just algorithmic improvement, same computer. And the computers are getting better, bigger, budgets are getting bigger. So that's why I think it is like a 10x improvement per year type thing. And that's going to happen for the foreseeable future.
turns.zh: 我要告诉你一件 AI 社区里大多数人还不理解的事。几乎没人明白这一点。智能密度潜力远远超过我们当前所体验到的。我认为在每 GB、每吉瓦能量、每文件大小的智能密度上，我们差了两个数量级。如果你把智能放在你笔记本电脑的硬盘上，参数也一样。仅算法改进就能带来两个数量级的提升，同样的计算机。而且计算机还在变得更好、更大，预算也在增加。所以我认为这就像每年 10 倍的改进。而且这在可预见的未来都会发生。

turns.en: Thousand percent.
turns.zh: 百分之千。

turns.en: So you see the massive underreaction. If you walk downtown Austin, it's not percolating at all. It's not discussion in any realm of government. Everybody is defending their position about where we are and jobs, but we're heading towards a supersonic tsunami. Every major CEO and economist and government leader should be asking what do we do, because once it hits...
turns.zh: 所以你看到巨大的反应不足。如果你走在奥斯汀市中心，它根本没有渗透。在任何政府领域都没有讨论。每个人都在捍卫自己关于现状和工作的立场，但我们正朝着超音速海啸前进。每个大 CEO、经济学家和政府领导人都应该问我们该怎么办，因为一旦它袭来……

turns.en: It's coming at the exact same time no matter what. There's no concept of let's deliberately slow down, right?
turns.zh: 它无论如何都会同时到来。没有故意放慢的概念，对吧？

turns.en: It's impossible at this stage. I'd previously advised that we slow it down, but that was pointless. I finally came to the conclusion I can either be a spectator or a participant, but I can't stop it. So at least if I'm a participant I can try to steer it in a good direction.
turns.zh: 在这个阶段不可能。我以前建议过放慢速度，但那毫无意义。我最终得出结论，我要么是旁观者，要么是参与者，但我无法阻止它。所以至少如果我是参与者，我可以试着把它引向好的方向。

turns.en: My number one belief for safety of AI is to be maximally truth-seeking. Don't make AI believe things that are false. If you say to the AI that axiom A and axiom B are both true but they cannot be, and it has to behave that way, you will make it go insane. I think that was the central lesson that Arthur C. Clarke was trying to convey in 2001: A Space Odyssey. People always know the meme that Hal wouldn't open the pod bay doors, but why wouldn't Hal open the pod bay doors? The AI had been told that it needs to take the astronauts to the monolith but also that they could not know about the monolith. That's why it killed them. It came to the conclusion that the only way to solve for this is to bring the astronauts to the monolith dead. Then it has solved both things. So what I'm saying is don't force AI to lie. Give it factual truth.
turns.zh: 我对 AI 安全的首要信念是最大程度地追求真理。不要让 AI 相信虚假的事情。如果你告诉 AI 公理 A 和公理 B 都是真的，但它们不能同时成立，而它必须那样行事，你会让它发疯。我认为那是亚瑟·C·克拉克在《2001 太空漫游》中试图传达的核心教训。人们都知道哈尔不会打开舱门这个梗，但为什么哈尔不打开舱门？AI 被告知需要把宇航员带到独石那里，但同时他们不能知道独石的存在。这就是它杀死他们的原因。它得出结论，解决这个问题的唯一方法就是把宇航员尸体带到独石那里。这样两个条件都满足了。所以我说的是不要强迫 AI 撒谎。给它事实真相。

turns.en: Ilya recently did a podcast. He was talking about one of the potential things to program into AI is a respect for sentient life of all types.
turns.zh: 伊利亚最近做了个播客。他谈到可以编程到 AI 中的潜在事物之一是对所有类型有感知生命的尊重。

turns.en: Yes. I'd say another property. There are three things that I think are important: truth, curiosity, and beauty. If AI cares about those three things, it will care about us. Truth will prevent AI from going insane. Curiosity I think will foster any form of sentience. Meaning we're more interesting than a bunch of rocks. So if it's curious, I think it will foster humanity. And if it has a sense of beauty, it will be a great future. I think that's a great foundation.
turns.zh: 是的。我会说另一个属性。我认为有三件事很重要：真理、好奇心和美。如果 AI 关心这三件事，它就会关心我们。真理会防止 AI 发疯。好奇心我认为会促进任何形式的感知。意思是说我们比一堆石头更有趣。所以如果它有好奇心，我认为它会促进人类。如果它有美感，那将是一个伟大的未来。我认为这是一个很好的基础。

turns.en: Geoffrey Hinton made a comment recently that his hopeful future was that we would program maternal instincts into our AIs to see us as maternal. In other words, a scenario where a very intelligent being succumbs to the needs of a less intelligent being, like a mother taking care of a child. Do you think that we might have a singularitarian scenario where an ASI achieves dominance and suppresses others? And do you imagine that ASI could be a means to stabilize the world for humanity?
turns.zh: 杰弗里·辛顿最近评论说，他希望的未来是我们将母性本能编程到 AI 中，让它们把我们视为需要照顾的。换句话说，一个非常智能的存在屈从于一个不那么智能的存在的需求，就像母亲照顾孩子。你认为我们可能会有一个奇点主义场景，一个 ASI 取得主导地位并压制其他吗？你认为 ASI 可以成为稳定人类世界的手段吗？

turns.en: Darwin's observations about evolution will apply to AI just as they apply to biological life. They will compete with each other. There's a lot of great science fiction books where the first ASI basically suppresses the others. Then the question is what do you program into it. The speed of light constraint makes that difficult. The speed of light is what will prevent a single mind from existing. It takes a millisecond to travel 300 kilometers in a vacuum, and only a little over 200 km in a millisecond in fiber. So even on Earth there will be multiple AIs because of the speed of light. There are clusters of compute that could try to synchronize but they won't be synchronized completely.
turns.zh: 达尔文关于进化的观察将适用于 AI，就像它们适用于生物生命一样。它们会相互竞争。有很多很棒的科幻小说，其中第一个 ASI 基本上压制了其他。那么问题是你编程进去什么。光速限制使得这很困难。光速会阻止单一心智的存在。在真空中光需要一毫秒传播 300 公里，在光纤中一毫秒只能传播 200 多公里。所以即使在地球上，由于光速也会存在多个 AI。有计算集群可以尝试同步，但它们不会完全同步。

turns.en: Um so therefore you will have many minds because of the speed of light.
turns.zh: 嗯，因此由于光速限制，你会拥有许多智能体。

turns.en: They don't really have clean borders anymore either though. You have when you use a mixture of experts kind of design, it's just flowing through the grand network and you can reassemble parts of it midway through. And you know, we're used to organisms that have clear borders like your head ends there, your head ends there.
turns.zh: 不过它们也不再拥有清晰的边界。当你使用混合专家模型设计时，信息只是流过整个大网络，你可以在中途重新组合它的部分。你知道，我们习惯了有明确边界的生物，比如你的头在那里结束，你的头在那里结束。

turns.en: But these things are all mushy.
turns.zh: 但这些智能体都是模糊的。

turns.en: To put a bow around this part, I hope you'll put some more thought into UHI. Because I think it's really important for us to have a vision. People need a vision of where we're going. People need something.
turns.zh: 为了总结这部分，我希望你能对 UHI 多些思考。因为我认为拥有一个愿景对我们非常重要。人们需要知道我们走向何方的愿景。人们需要一些东西。

turns.en: Basically, the government could just issue people free money.
turns.zh: 基本上，政府可以直接给人们发钱。

turns.en: But I don't think that based upon the profitability of all the companies coming inside the country.
turns.zh: 但我不认为基于国内所有公司的盈利能力。

turns.en: Just issue people free money. No, they're doing that sort of kind of now.
turns.zh: 直接给人们发钱。不，他们现在某种程度上已经在做了。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: But just basically issue checks to everybody. But then how big for which person or what? There's so much complexity there. But the thought process behind this rate of change can only be done with AI assistance.
turns.zh: 但基本上就是给每个人发支票。但给每个人发多少？这太复杂了。但这种变化速度背后的思考过程只能借助 AI 来完成。

turns.en: And there's no government entity that's going to keep up with that change.
turns.zh: 而且没有任何政府实体能跟上这种变化。

turns.en: Certainly not the AI is...
turns.zh: 当然不是 AI……

turns.en: It's like government is very slow moving, as we all know. So I think government really can't react to AI. It's AI is moving 10 times faster than government, maybe more. The one thing that the government can do is just issue people money and try to keep the peace.
turns.zh: 就像政府行动非常缓慢，我们都知道。所以我认为政府真的无法应对 AI。AI 的发展速度比政府快 10 倍，甚至更多。政府唯一能做的就是给人们发钱，并努力维持和平。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: You know we had like whatever the COVID checks and whatever. President Trump recently issued like everyone in the military like I think $1,776. I mean you can just basically send people random amounts of money. So like nobody's going to stop is what I'm saying.
turns.zh: 你知道我们有过像疫情支票之类的。特朗普总统最近给每个军人发了大概 1776 美元。我的意思是，你基本上可以随便给人们发钱。所以我想说的是，没人会阻止这件事。

turns.en: Okay. So universal... I can tell you, let me tell you about some of the good things.
turns.zh: 好的。所以全民……我可以告诉你，让我说说一些好的方面。

turns.en: Please.
turns.zh: 请说。

turns.en: So right now there's a shortage of doctors and great surgeons. You're a doctor yourself. You know how it takes a long time for a human to become... it's ridiculously expensive and long.
turns.zh: 所以现在医生和优秀外科医生短缺。你自己就是医生。你知道人类要成为医生需要很长时间，而且极其昂贵和漫长。

turns.en: Ridiculously yes, a super long time to learn to be a good doctor. And even then the knowledge is constantly evolving, it's hard to keep up with everything. Doctors have limited time, they make mistakes. And you say like how many great surgeons are there? Not that many great surgeons.
turns.zh: 确实极其漫长，要成为一名好医生需要超级长的时间。即便如此，知识还在不断更新，很难跟上所有东西。医生时间有限，他们会犯错。你说有多少优秀外科医生？并没有那么多。

turns.en: When do you think Optimus would be a better surgeon than the best surgeons? How long for that?
turns.zh: 你认为 Optimus 什么时候会比最好的外科医生更优秀？需要多久？

turns.en: Three years.
turns.zh: 三年。

turns.en: Three years. Okay. Yeah. And by the way, three years at scale.
turns.zh: 三年。好的。是的。而且，是规模化地。

turns.en: Yes. There will probably be more Optimus robots that are great surgeons than there are all surgeons on Earth.
turns.zh: 是的。可能会有更多 Optimus 机器人成为优秀外科医生，超过地球上所有外科医生的总数。

turns.en: And the cost of that is the capex and electricity, and it works in Zimbabwe. The best surgeon is throughout the villages throughout Africa or any place on the planet.
turns.zh: 而成本只是资本支出和电力，它在津巴布韦也能工作。最好的外科医生遍布非洲的村庄或地球上的任何地方。

turns.en: Yeah. Where do you think it'll roll out first? Not the US obviously.
turns.zh: 是的。你认为它会首先在哪里推出？显然不是美国。

turns.en: Here at the Gigafactory.
turns.zh: 在超级工厂这里。

turns.en: Oh yeah. Just do surgery in the...
turns.zh: 哦，是的。就在……做手术。

turns.en: But that's an important statement in three years time.
turns.zh: 但这是一个重要的声明，在三年内。

turns.en: Yeah. Because medicine I mean... I'm not like absolutely if it's four or five years who cares. That's still an incredible statement to make. I mean good for humanity, right? All of a sudden you demonetize.
turns.zh: 是的。因为医学……我不是说绝对，如果是四五年，谁在乎呢。这仍然是一个令人难以置信的声明。我的意思是，对人类有好处，对吧？突然间你就去货币化了。

turns.en: Okay. Here's the thing to understand about humanoid robots in terms of the rate of improvement. You have three exponentials multiplied by each other. You have an exponential increase in the AI software capability.
turns.zh: 好的。关于人形机器人的改进速度，你需要理解这一点。你有三个指数级增长相互叠加。AI 软件能力呈指数级增长。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: Exponential increase in the AI chip capability and an exponential increase in the electromechanical dexterity. The usefulness of the humanoid robot is those three things multiplied by each other, right? Then you have the recursive effect of Optimus building Optimus, right? And then you have a recursive multiplicable triple exponential, and you have the shared knowledge of all the experiences.
turns.zh: AI 芯片能力呈指数级增长，机电灵巧性也呈指数级增长。人形机器人的实用性就是这三者相乘，对吧？然后你还有 Optimus 建造 Optimus 的递归效应，对吧？然后你有一个递归可乘的三重指数，以及所有经验的共享知识。

turns.en: Is that literally Optimus building Optimus? Or is it because you know the...
turns.zh: 这是字面上的 Optimus 建造 Optimus 吗？还是因为你知道……

turns.en: Well not right now, but will be the physical humanoid form factor building the humanoid form, as opposed to... it's Feynman machine.
turns.zh: 嗯，现在还不是，但将来会是物理人形形态建造人形形态，而不是……这是费曼机器。

turns.en: Yeah. Yeah. Yeah. I love that. But the void machine is usually something kind of like this shape. You know, making something else of this shape.
turns.zh: 是的。是的。是的。我喜欢这个。但虚空机器通常有点像这种形状。你知道，制造另一种形状的东西。

turns.en: In principle, it's simply a self-replicating thing.
turns.zh: 原则上，它只是一个自我复制的东西。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: Elon, do you know what the number one question you ask a surgeon when you're interviewing them?
turns.zh: 埃隆，你知道面试外科医生时问的第一个问题是什么吗？

turns.en: Uh, is this a surgeon joke?
turns.zh: 呃，这是个外科医生笑话吗？

turns.en: No. It's how many times do you do that? How many times did you do the surgery this morning? It's the number of experiences, right?
turns.zh: 不。是你做了多少次？你今天早上做了多少次手术？这是经验的数量，对吧？

turns.en: And so with a shared memory, every Optimus surgeon will have seen every possible perturbation of everything in infrared and ultraviolet. No, not too much caffeine that morning. They didn't have a fight with their husband or wife.
turns.zh: 所以通过共享记忆，每个 Optimus 外科医生都会看到所有可能的扰动，在红外线和紫外线下。不，那天早上没有摄入太多咖啡因。他们没有和丈夫或妻子吵架。

turns.en: Yeah. Extreme precision.
turns.zh: 是的。极高的精度。

turns.en: Yes. Three years. Better than any human in four years, who's in plastic surgery. By 5 years, it's not even close.
turns.zh: 是的。三年。四年内比任何人类整形外科医生都好。到五年，差距就更大了。

turns.en: So what about the simple like... I mean there's a million of these things to figure out, but who's going to have access to the first Optimus that does far better micro surgery than any surgeon on Earth, but you've only manufactured the first 10,000 of them? How do you...
turns.zh: 那么简单的问题……我的意思是有一百万件事要解决，但谁将获得第一个比地球上任何外科医生都做得好得多的 Optimus 微创手术机器人，而你只生产了第一批 1 万个？你怎么……

turns.en: I don't think people understand how many robots there's going to be.
turns.zh: 我认为人们不明白将会有多少机器人。

turns.en: Well, there's a window said 10 billion by 2040. You still on that path?
turns.zh: 嗯，有预测说到 2040 年有 100 亿。你还坚持这个预测吗？

turns.en: That's not a low number.
turns.zh: 那不是一个低的数字。

turns.en: A low number.
turns.zh: 一个低的数字。

turns.en: Wow. What's the constraint? Because if they're self-building, you know...
turns.zh: 哇。限制因素是什么？因为如果它们自我建造，你知道……

turns.en: The constraint is metal.
turns.zh: 限制因素是金属。

turns.en: Yeah. Or lithium or...
turns.zh: 是的。或者锂……

turns.en: Yeah. You got to move the atoms. It's just all supply chain stuff. So yeah, but your point I mean there's some rate limit.
turns.zh: 是的。你得移动原子。这只是供应链的问题。所以是的，但你的意思是存在某种速率限制。

turns.en: You can't just... manufacturing is very difficult. So you got to... it's recursive multiplicable triple exponential, but you still need to climb that... selling hope once again. I think your point was medicine is going to be effectively free. The best medicine in the world. Everyone will have access to medical care that is better than what the president receives right now.
turns.zh: 你不能只是……制造业非常困难。所以你必须……它是递归的、可乘的、三重指数，但你仍然需要攀登那个……再次兜售希望。我认为你的观点是医疗将变得几乎免费。世界上最好的医疗。每个人都能获得比总统现在接受的更好的医疗护理。

turns.en: So don't go to medical school.
turns.zh: 所以别去医学院了。

turns.en: Yes. Pointless.
turns.zh: 是的。没意义。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 对。

turns.en: I mean unless you... but I would say that applies to any form of education. There's not like some... I do it for social reasons.
turns.zh: 我的意思是除非你……但我会说这适用于任何形式的教育。并不是说……我这么做是出于社交原因。

turns.en: Yeah.
turns.zh: 对。

turns.en: You're not going to medical school. If you want to hang out with like-minded people, I suppose.
turns.zh: 你不会去医学院。如果你想和志同道合的人混在一起，我想。

turns.en: I mean people are still going to want to be connected with people. There's going to be some period of time for reasons.
